meaning: to see; to look; to watch; to meet up; to read

expressions.english: something to see/look/watch
to read a book
to watch a movie
to babysit
expressions.notes:
애 = kid, child; baby
애 is the contracted form of 아이.

expressions.english: to taste (to see if the food is too salty or too bland)
Well, look here.
expressions.notes:
자 = Well (interjection to start a sentence)
여기 = here, this place

expressions.english: Look at this.
expressions.notes:
이것 = this; this thing
좀 = a little; please
-(으)세요 = (polite) imperative

expressions.english: I have never seen anything like this before.
expressions.notes:
이런 = N + like this
이런 is the adjective form of 이렇다.
이런 거 = something like this
거 = thing, stuff; object
처음 = first; for the first time

expressions.english: I have to take a test tomorrow.
expressions.notes:
내일 = tomorrow
시험 = test, examination
-어/여/아야 돼다 = to have to + V

expressions.english: Why do you keep looking at your watch?
expressions.notes:
왜 = why; for what reason; how come
시계 = clock, watch
자구 = repeatedly, again and again

expressions.korean: 이 책 나중에 꼭 볼 거예요.
expressions.english: I will read this book later, definitely.
expressions.notes:
이 = this
책 = for sure; at any cost; certainly; definitely; make sure to (do something); tight
나중에 = later

expressions.english: I burned my tongue while I was tasting the food.
expressions.notes:
맛 = taste
맛을 보다 = to taste
혀 = tongue
데다 = to burn oneself

expressions.english: Yesterday, I watched a movie with my friend.
The more I see it, the more I like it.
expressions.notes:
-(으)면 -(으)ㄹ수록 = the more..., the more...
마음 = heart; mind
들다 = to enter; to be categorized as; to be in
마음에 들다 = to like

expressions.korean: 우리 다음 주에 꼭 한번 봐요.
expressions.english: Let's make sure to meet up next week.
expressions.notes:
우리 = we; us
주 = week
한번 = one time, once
한번 -어/여/아요 = Let's + V + sometime in the future

expressions.english: I saw him by chance yesterday.
expressions.notes:
우연히 = by chance
그 = the; that
사람 = person, people

expressions.english: I couldn't see it because it was too dark.
expressions.notes:
너무 = too much, excessively; very
어둡다 = to be dark
-어/여/아서 = because, since
-(으)ㄹ 수 없다 = to be unable to + V, can't

expressions.english: As soon as I saw it, I liked it so much that I bought it.
expressions.notes:
자마자 = as soon as + S + V
사다 = to buy

expressions.korean: 시간이 없어서 아직 그 영화 못 봤어요.
expressions.english: I haven't had time, so I haven't been able to watch that movie yet.
expressions.notes:
시간 = time; hour
아직 = (not) yet
못 = can't
없다 = to not have; to not be there; to not exist

expressions.korean: 친구가 그 드라마 재미없다고 해서 안 봤어요.
expressions.english: A friend of mine told me that the drama was not interesting, so I didn't watch it.
expressions.notes:
드라마 = TV drama, soap opera
-다고 해서 = since someone says/said ...
인 = not
재미없다 = to be not funny; to be not interesting; to be boring
